doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_powerof2_raw2 (mpfr/src/powerof2.c L40-L49). The C function inspects a raw limb array and returns 1 iff the top limb is exactly MPFR_LIMB_HIGHBIT (= 1 << (GMP_NUMB_BITS-1)) AND all lower limbs are zero — i.e. the mantissa, viewed as a sequence of bits, has exactly one bit set (the MSB of the top limb). In the locked schema where `mant` is a single bigint MSB-aligned to `prec` bits, that condition is equivalent to `mant === 2n ** (prec - 1n)`: the MSB at position prec-1 is the only set bit. The TS port hoists the predicate to the bigint level — no limb-array detour — which is faithful at the I/O contract level (Law 3) while idiomatic on the TS surface. Class is 'misc' for the 1000ms budget; the actual implementation is O(1) modulo bigint shift cost. The C-side caller (mpfr_powerof2_raw, L30-L38) requires the caller to assert `MPFR_IS_PURE_FP(x)` — i.e. the input must be a normal value. The TS port matches: callers should only invoke this on a mantissa drawn from a 'normal'-kind MPFR. The function does NOT validate the MSB-alignment invariant beyond what's necessary to answer the predicate; an out-of-range mantissa silently returns false.
divergence_from_c: C takes (const mp_limb_t *xp, mp_size_t xn) — a raw limb array pointer and limb count. TS takes (mant: bigint, prec: bigint) — the same information at a higher abstraction level (the bigint encodes the same bit pattern; prec gives the bit-position of the MSB rather than a limb count). C returns `int` (0/1); TS returns `boolean`. The C function asserts nothing structurally (it just reads the limbs); the TS port likewise does no bounds checking on `mant` — callers passing a mantissa with no bit set at position prec-1 get `false` (since the C function would also return false for an all-zero limb array). No exception path; pure predicate. NaN/Inf/zero MPFR values should NOT be fed in (the C-side caller mpfr_powerof2_raw asserts MPFR_IS_PURE_FP via the disabled MPFR_ASSERTN at L36) — the TS port mirrors by being a substrate-level helper that callers use only on normal-kind mantissas.
refs: mpfr/src/powerof2.c L40-L49 — the C reference body.
mpfr/src/mpfr-impl.h L1301 — MPFR_LIMB_HIGHBIT = 1 << (GMP_NUMB_BITS - 1) — the top-limb pattern the C side compares against.
mpfr/src/mpfr-impl.h L972-L980 — MPFR_PREC2LIMBS / MPFR_LIMB_SIZE: limb count for a given precision.
src/core.ts L93-L135 — locked MPFR value model; mantissa MSB-aligned to prec bits.
src/internal/mpfr/round_raw.ts — sibling substrate helper convention (no core.ts import, idiomatic bigint surface).

CLAUDE.md Law 3 — faithful substrate, idiomatic surface. The bigint-level expression `mant === 2n ** (prec - 1n)` is the idiomatic form of the C limb-walk; bit-for-bit equivalent at the I/O contract level.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' — mpn limbs are little-endian; the C loop walks from xn-1 down to 0, but the bigint expression sidesteps the iteration entirely.
